Empires and Puzzles Costumed Emilio Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Red Hero (Starfall Circus)
Emilio is back with a frog mask and modern stats. Is the 'Great Frog Tamer' a top-tier threat or just a shiny coat of paint on an old trick? We analyze his new form.
Costumed Emilio Review: The Great Frog Tamer Returns
Costumed Emilio: From Phoenixes to Frogs

Emilio has returned with a costume that swaps his dignified top hat for... a frog mask? "The Great Frog Tamer" (yes, really) brings a massive stat overhaul to this classic Very Fast hero. Released in April 2025, this costume attempts to modernize Emilio's kit, shifting him from a pure support survivor to a slightly more aggressive disruptor. This review focuses on his
, analyzing whether the stat injection and new passive are enough to make him a meta contender again.

If the base version was "Gothic Ringmaster," the costume is "Psychedelic Swamp Circus." The green frog mask and vibrant colors are a stark departure from the original's red and black palette. It’s certainly... memorable. While some might miss the dapper skeleton look, the sheer absurdity of "The Great Frog Tamer" fits the chaotic energy of the Starfall Circus perfectly. It’s weird, it’s colorful, and it definitely stands out in a lineup.

The costume brings Emilio roaring into the present day. With a base Power of 1211 (compared to the original 809), he is no longer a glass cannon—he's a reinforced steel cannon. His HP jumps from a paltry 1400 to a massive 2336, and his Defense skyrockets to 1164. This is the single most important upgrade. He can finally take a hit from modern snipers like Turundh or Jwala without instantly vaporizing, allowing him to actually use his Very Fast skills.
The core mechanic remains the same (Burn + conditional effects), but the numbers have shifted:
The burn damage has significantly increased (715 vs 415). This makes him a legitimate threat over time rather than just an annoyance.
The healing has dropped (450 vs 549). This is a notable nerf to his sustain potential. The costume pushes him more towards a "Damage Dealer/Support" hybrid rather than a "Healer/Support."
The 3-turn ailment immunity is still here, and with his new bulk, he’s much more likely to keep applying it.
Very Fast. This is still his defining feature.
715 Burn over 5 turns = 143 damage/turn. This is nearly double the base version's output. While still a DoT, it puts actual pressure on the enemy team.
150 HP per turn. It’s barely a scratch compared to modern burst healers, but it mitigates chip damage.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The costume introduces a new passive that adds a layer of "don't touch me" to his kit.
The cleanse chance remains excellent. Combined with his Very Fast speed, he is incredibly hard to lock down.

When an ailment is cleansed from him, a copy is thrown onto a random enemy. This is hilarious and potentially devastating. If you cleanse a defense down or a mindless attack from him, he just hands it right back to the enemy. It turns the Starfall Family cleanse into a weapon.
fits his new, more aggressive profile. It boosts his burn damage further, leaning into his role as a whittler.
The base Emilio was a legend in his time. The Costume attempts to recapture that glory by fixing his biggest flaw: his stats.
The stat boost puts him on par with 2024/2025 heroes. He is no longer "outdated" in terms of survivability.

He isn't the game-breaking monster he was in 2021 because immunity is more common now (Hathor, etc.), but a Very Fast immunity generator with 2800+ HP (LB2) is always useful. He excels in fast-paced matches where preventing ailments early is key.
Costume wins on raw stats, hands down.
Base (Druid) offers minions for survival; Costume (Wizard) offers Jinx for extra damage.
Costume has the "Copy Ailment" passive; Base has nothing.

The stats are too good to ignore. The drop in healing is worth the massive increase in bulk and burn damage. The only exception is if you desperately need the higher HoT and are confident you can keep the fragile Base form alive (perhaps with the costume bonus stats applied).
With his new bulk and "Wizard" class, you have more flexibility.
Ensures he stays alive to keep the immunity loop going. His burn is strong enough without full attack emblems.
Misses out on maximizing the Jinx talent.
Maximizes DoT damage and Jinx proc chance.
Still risky against top-tier snipers, even with improved stats.
path. His primary value is utility (Immunity), not damage. If he's dead, he provides zero immunity.
Finally has the HP to survive in the current meta.
The increased burn damage actually hurts now.
The "Copy Ailment" passive discourages enemies from spamming ailments on him (or punishes them when he cleanses them).
450 HP over 3 turns is very low for a modern "healer." Do not bring him as your solo healer.
Still relies on the burn condition to reach full potential.
Jinx is less defensively useful than the Druid's minions.
A bulky, Very Fast immunity provider is a nightmare for the defense to deal with.
Great for countering ailment-heavy defenses (e.g., against heavy DoT teams).
Incredible here. He provides immunity and damage, and the reduced healing doesn't matter since healing is disabled anyway.
Costumed Emilio works best as a "Secondary Support/Attacker."
. Combine his burn with heavy poison/damage.
. Double immunity coverage. When one is down, the other is up.
. If they cleanse his burn, he loses his conditional trigger for the next cast.
. If your team needs big heals to survive, C. Emilio won't cut it.
If you pull the costume, level it immediately. The stats alone make him a carry for early/mid-game content. Use him to breeze through difficult map stages by blocking boss ailments.

He is a luxury tool. Excellent for depth in War teams, especially for Rush. He likely won't replace your top-tier main defense heroes, but having a Very Fast immunity hero with 1200+ Power is a card you'll want in your deck for specific counters.
"I don't think this hero is usable at all... Do not double limit broke this guy." Some players felt the damage increase didn't justify the lower healing.
Others argue that in the modern meta,
hero with 1400 HP (base) is unusable, so the Costume is the
way to use Emilio, regardless of the healing nerf.
He's not the "must-have" he was in 2021, but the costume breathes valuable life into a dusty roster spot.

Costumed Emilio is a successful modernization. He trades a bit of his supportive soul (healing) for a thicker skin and a sharper bite. While he might not be the undisputed king of reds anymore, "The Great Frog Tamer" proves that you can teach an old dog (or frog) new tricks—mostly by feeding him steroids until his stats match the current year.
A revitalized classic that trades healing for survivability and disruption.
A deep dive into Costumed Emilio, the revitalized Very Fast Fire hero. We analyze his massive stat boost, new Wizard class, and whether his 'Frog Tamer' persona brings him back to the meta.
